us-politics.headline: US Warplanes Shot Down Over Iran, Crew Member Missing
us-politics.body:
Two U.S. fighter jets and two helicopters were hit by Iranian fire during ongoing military operations, with at least one crew member still missing. Recovery efforts remain active. The losses mark a serious escalation in what has become an undeclared war with Iran.

On Capitol Hill, Republican Senator John Curtis called for ending offensive operations without a formal declaration of war, while Rep. Nancy Mace voiced concern about further escalation and ground troop deployment. NPR notes Trump's wartime rhetoric departs sharply from the measured language of past presidents. A Democrat also condemned prediction market Polymarket for taking bets on the missing pilot's fate.

sources.title: What to know about US fighter jets hit in Iran war
2 US helicopters hit during recovery efforts in Iran
GOP senator calls to end Iran operations without formal declaration of war
Two U.S. warplanes shot down; search ongoing in Iran for 1 missing crew member
Mace ‘concerned about escalation’ in Iran war
Democrat rips Polymarket for taking bets on fate of US pilot shot down over Iran
How Trump's wartime rhetoric differs from past presidents and why that matters

us-politics.headline: Trump Budget Draws Fire From Both Parties
us-politics.body:
Trump's proposed federal budget includes a $10 billion fund for Washington D.C. construction and beautification, cuts to TSA funding with mandated privatization at small airports, and a restructured defense spending plan. The proposal has drawn criticism across party lines.

Republican Senator Susan Collins publicly knocked the funding cuts, signaling the budget faces resistance within the GOP. Senate Democrats separately pressed the Treasury Department over its decision to add Trump's name to U.S. currency. The budget's defense component increases military spending overall, but critics argue the domestic cuts are too deep.

sources.title: Susan Collins knocks funding cuts in White House budget proposal
What to know about Trump’s defense budget proposal
Senate Democrats press Treasury Department on decision to add Trump name to currency
Trump proposes $10B fund for D.C. construction, beautification projects
Trump budget would cut TSA funding, require privatization at small airports

us-politics.headline: DOJ Shake-Up: Blanche In, Epstein Questions Linger
us-politics.body:
Todd Blanche, Trump's former personal attorney, has taken over as the top official at the Justice Department following Pam Bondi's departure. Blanche stated there is no evidence in the Epstein files that Jeffrey Epstein worked as a spy. Mace said she is not backing away from pushing for Bondi to testify on the matter.

Meanwhile, Trump is weighing further Cabinet changes but wants to avoid a broad shake-up, according to the Washington Post. FBI Director Kash Patel and Army Secretary McCormick separately warned that foreign terror threats inside the U.S. grew during the Biden years. Senator Thom Tillis also set a Jan. 6 red line for any future attorney general nominee.

sources.title: Trump weighs more Cabinet changes, but wants to avoid ‘massive shake-up’
Todd Blanche takes on DOJ’s top job and the burden of Trump’s expectations
Blanche: No evidence in Epstein files he was a spy
Patel, McCormick warn foreign terror threats inside US grew during Biden years
Tillis sets Jan. 6 red line on attorney general nominee
Mace ‘not backing away’ from Bondi testifying on Epstein

us-politics.headline: Voting Rights, College Sports, and State Battles With Trump
us-politics.body:
Oregon's attorney general sued the Trump administration over its executive order restricting vote-by-mail, calling it a source of "confusion, chaos and distrust." A coalition of Democratic-led states filed a separate lawsuit on the same order. Trump's directive would require proof of citizenship for federal election registration, a requirement critics say will suppress turnout.

On a different front, Trump signed another executive order targeting college sports, framed as an effort to protect competitive fairness. Colorado lawyers reported that the state's court e-filing system now requires them to certify they will not assist ICE, a move Fox News highlighted as a direct conflict with federal immigration enforcement. Vice President Vance was tapped to lead a new anti-fraud initiative targeting what the White House calls taxpayer theft in Democratic-run states.

sources.title: Trump again orders changes to ‘save’ college sports
‘Confusion, chaos and distrust’: Oregon challenges Trump’s order restricting vote-by-mail
Colorado lawyers say court e-file system now makes them certify they won’t assist ICE
Democratic-led states sue over Trump mail-in ballot order
Vance tapped as ‘fraud czar’ as Trump targets blue states over taxpayer theft

au-politics.headline: Nine charged as sunken trawler exposes major drug conspiracy
au-politics.body:
Nine people face charges over an alleged plot to import tonnes of cocaine and methamphetamine into Australia using a so-called mother ship operating in Australian waters. The operation unravelled after a trawler sank, alerting Victoria Police to the alleged syndicate. Investigators say the scale of the alleged importation is significant.

The arrests follow a joint law enforcement operation and mark one of the larger drug conspiracy cases in recent memory. Details of how the vessel came to sink, and what evidence it yielded, remain central to the prosecution case. No convictions have been recorded and all accused are presumed innocent.

sources.title: Nine charged over alleged conspiracy to import tonnes of cocaine and meth via ‘mother ship’ in Australian waters
Sunken trawler alerts police to alleged drug-smuggling plan

au-politics.headline: One Nation leaves SA Liberals clinging to opposition status
au-politics.body:
South Australia's Liberal Party is fighting to retain its formal status as the opposition after One Nation's presence in the upper house complicated the parliamentary arithmetic. Analysts describe the situation as having almost no precedent in the state's political history. The Liberals need to hold their position to maintain resources and speaking rights that come with official opposition standing.

The development reflects a broader pattern of minor parties disrupting traditional two-party dynamics in state legislatures. One Nation's growing footprint in South Australian politics adds pressure on the Liberals to consolidate their base ahead of the next election cycle.

sources.title: 'So few precedents': One Nation leaves SA Liberals clinging to opposition status

au-politics.headline: Iran shoots down US jet, raising stakes with Trump administration
au-politics.body:
Iran has downed a United States aircraft, an incident analysts say will reverberate through American public opinion in three distinct ways. The episode risks becoming a defining foreign policy test for the Trump administration, which faces pressure to respond without triggering a broader conflict. Commentators warn the incident carries echoes of past moments where US credibility was seen to be on the line.

The shoot-down raises immediate questions about rules of engagement and the posture of US forces in the region. How Washington responds in the coming days will shape both diplomatic and domestic political calculations.

sources.title: Iran's downing of a US jet will shock Americans in three ways

au-politics.headline: Fuel costs bite caravanners as EV novated lease interest grows
au-politics.body:
Long-term caravanners say rising fuel prices are forcing itinerary changes and financial stress, with some comparing the disruption to pandemic-era lockdowns. The crisis is hitting travellers in remote areas hardest, where fuel alternatives are scarce and distances between stops are long.

Meanwhile, interest in electric vehicles is growing through novated leases, which allow employees to pay for an EV using pre-tax salary. Advocates say the arrangement offers meaningful savings, though critics note it primarily benefits higher-income earners with access to employer salary-packaging schemes. The contrast between the two stories points to an uneven energy transition playing out across different segments of Australian life.

sources.title: Another 'lockdown': How long-term caravanners are dealing with the fuel crisis
More people are using this 'secret deal' to get an EV. Here's what to know

world.headline: US-Iran War Escalates as Hormuz Tensions Grip Region
world.body: Iran shot down two US warplanes and the conflict is now rippling across the wider Middle East, with Israel striking bridges in Lebanon and a US-Israeli strike on an Iranian bridge leaving survivors describing scenes of terror. The WHO reports more than 20 attacks on Iranian healthcare facilities since March 1. A French-owned ship successfully passed through the Strait of Hormuz, but questions over whether force will be used to reopen the waterway remain live. The UAE's Habshan gas facility was also struck in an intercepted attack, killing at least one person, underscoring how the conflict is threatening regional energy infrastructure.

sources.title: Iran war live: Tehran downs 2 US warplanes; Israel bombs Lebanon bridges
Will force be used to reopen Strait of Hormuz?
Survivors of Iran bridge attack recall terror of US-Israeli strike
More than 20 attacks on Iranian healthcare facilities since March 1: WHO
At least one killed at UAE’s Habshan gas facility after intercepted attack
French-owned ship passes through Strait of Hormuz
What we know about the US fighter jet shot down in Iran

world.headline: Gaza and Lebanon Christians Mark Good Friday Under Fire
world.body: Palestinian Christians in Gaza and Lebanese Christians observed Good Friday as Israeli military operations continued around them. In Jerusalem's Old City, monks held Easter prayers in streets emptied by the conflict. The scenes highlight how the war has hollowed out Christian communities in some of Christianity's most historically significant places, with worshippers unable to gather freely during one of the faith's most important observances.

sources.title: Palestinian Christians in Gaza mark Good Friday
Christians in Lebanon observe Good Friday under Israeli attacks
Monks hold Easter prayers in deserted Old City of Jerusalem

world.headline: Cuba Frees 2,000 Prisoners as US Pressure Mounts
world.body: Cuba has begun releasing more than 2,000 prisoners in what appears to be a direct response to sustained pressure from Washington. The releases mark a notable shift for Havana, which has long resisted external demands on its domestic governance. The move raises questions about what, if anything, the Cuban government expects in return and whether it signals a broader recalibration of relations with the United States.

sources.title: Cuba begins releasing more than 2,000 prisoners as US pressure mounts
Cuba releases over 2,000 prisoners amid mounting US pressure

world.headline: Artemis II Crew Sends Back Stunning Earth Images
world.body: NASA's Artemis II crew captured striking photographs of Earth during the mission's lunar trajectory, with both NASA and the crew describing the images as spectacular. The photos are the first taken by astronauts from that distance since the Apollo era. The mission marks a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon, and the images have drawn wide attention as a rare human perspective on the planet from deep space.

sources.title: Artemis II crew take 'spectacular' image of Earth
NASA releases stunning first photos of Earth from Artemis II moon mission

ai.headline: Anthropic's Big Week: Biotech Deal, PAC, and Claude Crackdown
ai.body:
Anthropic is moving fast on multiple fronts. The company reportedly acquired biotech startup Coefficient Bio for $400 million, launched a new political action committee to expand its Washington presence, and rolled out new Claude subscription bundles - all within the same news cycle. Private market investors are paying close attention, though a looming SpaceX IPO could suck oxygen from the room.

On the product side, Anthropic effectively cut off OpenClaw, a popular third-party Claude client, by requiring its users to pay extra on top of existing subscriptions. The move drew immediate backlash. OpenClaw's security practices are also under scrutiny, with researchers warning users to assume their credentials may be compromised.

sources.title: Anthropic is having a moment in the private markets; SpaceX could spoil the party
Anthropic essentially bans OpenClaw from Claude by making subscribers pay extra
Extra usage credit for Claude to celebrate usage bundles launch (Pro, Max, Team)
Tell HN: Anthropic no longer allowing Claude Code subscriptions to use OpenClaw
OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: Reports
Anthropic ramps up its political activities with a new PAC

ai.headline: OpenAI Reshuffles Leadership as Key Executives Step Back
ai.body:
OpenAI is reorganizing at the top. COO Brad Lightcap is moving into a new "special projects" role, while Fidji Simo - brought in less than a year ago to run the applications business - is taking medical leave. The company's AGI research lead is also stepping away.

The simultaneous departures raise questions about internal stability at a company already navigating a high-stakes transition to a for-profit structure. OpenAI has not detailed what Lightcap's special projects role will cover.

sources.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
OpenAI’s AGI boss is taking a leave of absence
OpenAI’s Fidji Simo Is Taking Medical Leave Amid an Executive Shake-Up

ai.headline: AI's Infrastructure Problem: Tariffs, Gas Plants, and Hostile Neighbors
ai.body:
The AI industry's data center ambitions are running into hard reality. Trump's own tariffs are driving up construction costs and slowing the buildout his administration championed. Meanwhile, companies are turning to massive natural gas plants to meet power demands, a bet that carries both environmental and regulatory risk.

Public opinion isn't helping either. Surveys show communities would sooner accept an Amazon warehouse next door than a data center. Iran strikes knocked Amazon's cloud infrastructure offline in Bahrain and Dubai, a reminder of how exposed this concentrated infrastructure can be.

sources.title: Iran strikes leave Amazon availability zones "hard down" in Bahrain and Dubai
Trump ignores biggest reasons his AI data center buildout is failing
AI companies are building huge natural gas plants to power data centers. What could go wrong?
People would rather have an Amazon warehouse in their backyard than a data center

ai.headline: Artemis II Heads to the Moon as NASA Budget Faces Cuts
ai.body:
The Artemis II crew is en route to the Moon and, by all accounts, the mission is running smoothly. The astronauts have already sent back striking images of Earth from deep space. It is a genuine milestone for NASA and human spaceflight.

The timing is awkward. The Trump administration has simultaneously proposed steep cuts to NASA's budget, putting future missions in doubt even as this one unfolds. Elon Musk, whose SpaceX is central to the Artemis program, is separately drawing attention for reportedly requiring banks working on the SpaceX IPO to purchase Grok subscriptions.

sources.title: Trump proposes steep cut to NASA budget as astronauts head for the Moon
As Artemis II zooms to the Moon, everything seems to be going swimmingly
Elon Musk insists banks working on SpaceX IPO must buy Grok subscriptions
Artemis II crew take “spectacular” image of Earth

btc.headline: Circle Faces Heat Over $285M Hack and Compliance Failures
btc.body:
Circle is under serious pressure after the $285 million Drift protocol hack exposed the stablecoin issuer's reluctance to freeze stolen USDC. Critics argue Circle's inaction undermines the core value proposition of a regulated, freezable stablecoin. The backlash is compounding.

On-chain investigator ZachXBT separately accused Circle of $420 million in compliance failures dating back to 2022, alleging the company repeatedly failed to act on illicit fund flows. Together, the two cases paint a damaging picture of a company preparing for a public listing while struggling to defend its compliance credibility. Regulators and institutional partners will be watching closely.

sources.title: Circle under fire after $285 million Drift hack over inaction to freeze stolen USDC
ZachXBT accuses Circle of $420M in 'compliance failures' since 2022

btc.headline: Schwab Eyes Spot Crypto Trading as Bitcoin Dips Below $70K
btc.body:
Charles Schwab plans to launch direct spot trading for Bitcoin and Ether in the first half of 2026, a move that signals mainstream brokerage firms are no longer willing to cede crypto market share to dedicated exchanges. The announcement comes as Bitcoin slips below $70,000, rattling corporate treasury strategies built around the asset.

The price dip is creating visible tension among Bitcoin treasury holders, with some firms breaking from the aggressive accumulation playbook popularized by Strategy. A former hedge fund manager also flagged that Bitcoin may be underpricing the risk of a prolonged Iran conflict, adding a geopolitical dimension to an already uncertain macro backdrop.

sources.title: Schwab plans spot bitcoin, ether trading launch in first half of 2026
Is Bitcoin mispricing a prolonged Iran war? Ex-hedge fund manager weighs in
Crypto Biz: Bitcoin treasuries break ranks as BTC dips below $70K

btc.headline: Coinbase Trust Charter Draws Banking Opposition, Regulatory Scrutiny
btc.body:
US community banks are pushing back against the OCC's approval of a trust charter for Coinbase, arguing it grants the crypto exchange bank-like privileges without equivalent regulatory obligations. The opposition reflects a broader anxiety in traditional finance about crypto firms gaining institutional footholds through regulatory side doors.

Meanwhile, Cambodia's lawmakers are moving in a different direction, proposing severe prison sentences for crypto scammers in a sign that emerging markets are tightening enforcement frameworks. Across both stories, the central tension is the same - who gets to operate in financial markets, and on what terms.

sources.title: US community banks oppose OCC's approval of Coinbase trust charter
Cambodian lawmakers propose severe prison time for crypto scammers

btc.headline: Ethereum Staking Milestone Nears as L2 Scaling Debate Heats Up
btc.body:
The Ethereum Foundation is closing in on its 70,000 staked ETH target, a milestone that reinforces the network's shift toward a staking-driven security model. The progress comes as Offchain Labs argues that Ethereum Layer 2 networks need more responsive, dynamic fee pricing to handle the transaction volumes required for mainstream adoption. Static pricing, the firm contends, is a ceiling on scale.

Binance dominated crypto derivatives in Q1 while Hyperliquid cracked the top ten for the first time, according to CoinGlass data. The ranking underscores how execution quality and platform reliability are becoming the competitive battleground - a theme echoed by analysts arguing that execution risk has replaced custody risk as the primary concern for serious traders.

sources.title: Ethereum Foundation nearly reaches 70,000 staked ETH goal
Ethereum L2s need responsive pricing to scale, says Offchain Labs
Binance led Q1 crypto derivatives as Hyperliquid cracked top 10: CoinGlass
Execution risk in crypto is the new custody risk

food.name: Miso-Glazed Leek and Walnut Tart
food.description: A buttery shortcrust tart filled with slow-caramelised leeks, toasted walnuts, and a white miso custard that walks the line between savoury and umami-sweet.
food.tip: Blind-bake the shell until fully golden before adding the filling — miso custard is wetter than it looks and will turn the base soggy if you skip this step.

exercise.name: The April Ladder
exercise.equipment: A single kettlebell (or heavy backpack), a timer
exercise.description: A ascending-rep ladder of kettlebell swings, push-ups, and reverse lunges — starting at 3 reps each and climbing by 2 every round until form breaks, then descending back down, making it both a strength and mental endurance session.

learn.description: Stochastic resonance is the counterintuitive phenomenon where adding a precise amount of random noise to a weak signal actually makes that signal easier to detect — used by the nervous systems of crayfish, exploited in medical devices for balance disorders, and studied in climate science to explain ice age cycles.
learn.why: It upends the instinct that noise is always the enemy of clarity, and once you understand it you start spotting its fingerprints everywhere from neuroscience to financial modelling — a genuinely mind-shifting Saturday rabbit hole.
